data.content:
## Handoff Capsule â€” SHIPPED

### Root cause
The interactive session watchdog in src/lib/agent-session-runtime-helpers.ts fired silently after `config.watchdogTimeoutMs` of inactivity. The setTimeout callback called `terminateProcessGroup(child)` with no console output and no lifecycle event. The resulting SIGTERM looked identical to a graceful process exit in logs â€” a `child_close code=0 aborted=false` with no breadcrumb. That silence caused operators investigating knot foolery-557d iteration 3 (abrupt ~10 min termination around 2026-04-17 07:34:09) to waste time looking for crashes when the real cause was the watchdog.

### Fix
Before calling `terminateProcessGroup`, the watchdog timeout callback now:
1. Emits `console.warn("[terminal-manager] [watchdog] timeout_fired pid=<pid> timeoutMs=<ms> msSinceLastEvent=<delta> lastEventType=<type|null> reason=timeout")`.
2. Invokes `config.onLifecycleEvent?.({ type: "watchdog_fired", timeoutMs, msSinceLastEvent, lastEventType })`.

### Files touched
- `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` â€” added `watchdog_fired` variant to `SessionRuntimeLifecycleEvent` union (lines ~83-89); added `watchdogArmedAt: number | null` to `SessionRuntimeState` (line ~137); initialized `watchdogArmedAt: null` in `createSessionRuntime` (line ~422).
- `src/lib/agent-session-runtime-helpers.ts` â€” added new `fireWatchdogTimeout(child, state, config, ms)` function (lines ~275-305) that performs the log+event emission and then terminates; updated `doResetWatchdog` (lines ~307-321) to stamp `state.watchdogArmedAt = Date.now()` before arming the timer and to delegate firing to `fireWatchdogTimeout`.
- `src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime-watchdog-fired.test.ts` â€” new unit test file with one test `emits watchdog_fired and warns before SIGTERM` using `vi.useFakeTimers()`, spying on `console.warn` and `process.kill`, asserting the warn message contains the required tag/fields, the lifecycle event is emitted with correct values, and the warn happens before SIGTERM via `invocationCallOrder`.

### Test that locks this down
`src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime-watchdog-fired.test.ts::runtime: watchdog_fired lifecycle::warns and emits event before SIGTERM`. If this test is ever removed or weakened the fix will silently regress; this is the canary.

### Why this won't regress
- The test asserts the exact warn tag `[terminal-manager] [watchdog]`, timeout_fired substring, pid=12345, timeoutMs=10000, reason=timeout, and lastEventType=assistant.
- The test asserts `warnSpy.mock.invocationCallOrder[0] < killSpy.mock.invocationCallOrder[0]`, so any refactor that moves the terminate call ahead of the warn fails loudly.
- The type system guarantees the `watchdog_fired` union variant â€” anyone deleting the variant in `SessionRuntimeLifecycleEvent` will hit a TS error at the callsite in `fireWatchdogTimeout`.

### Reference commit hashes
- Implementation: c1704a95 (fix(foolery-2782): log watchdog firing before SIGTERM)
- Merge on main: 1a99ec1c (Merge worktree-foolery-2782)

### Out-of-scope boundaries respected
- Did NOT touch onResult / handleResultEvent (knot foolery-a401).
- Did NOT touch terminateProcessGroup body (knot foolery-062f).
- Did NOT add msSinceLastStdout to child_close logs (knot foolery-e750).
- Did NOT remove the `fake-fixed-already` tag; preserved per instructions.
